title: psoralea imbricata
intro: Psoralea imbricata, also known as Wolf's Bane or Bishop's Weed, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Psoralea imbricata include its potential to treat skin conditions such as eczema and psoriasis, as well as its ability to lower cholesterol levels and reduce the risk of heart disease. Herbal preparations of Psoralea imbricata include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
